source: Closed state attorney-general settlement/AOD skeleton carrier pack drafted from retained public state-AG fixtures under datasets/real_world_transfer.
purpose: Constrain state-AG settlement and assurance compiles to recurring wrapper, party, authority, chronology, obligation, monetary, contact, and signature anatomy before attempting open-ended findings or liability substance.

lenses.purpose: Instrument wrapper, issuing authority, respondent, instrument number, and party roster.
Compact issue, investigation, resolution, effective-date, and signature-date chronology.
Compact prospective-relief, compliance, cooperation, and monetary-payment obligations.
Notice, report, payment, and correspondence channels.
Signature-block signing acts and signing capacities.

predicates.notes: Identifies one source-stated state-AG assurance, consent order, consent judgment, complaint, or settlement instrument. Companion carriers preserve parties, authorities, obligations, payments, contacts, and signatures.
Links one source-stated regulator, respondent, counsel, contact, registered agent, or signatory organization to the instrument.
Links an instrument or obligation subject to one source-stated legal citation with a compact structural citation role.
Preserves one compact source-stated event date relevant to the instrument or investigated matter, without causal narrative.

Preserves one compact source-stated prospective, reporting, compliance, cooperation, or notice obligation. For numbered agreement paragraphs, prefer source-anchored obligation_<paragraph_number> identifiers and do not copy a document-wide effective date into every obligation's due_or_effective_date slot.
Preserves one source-stated monetary payment obligation using compact amount, payment-kind, and timing atoms.
Preserves one source-stated notice, report, payment, or correspondence channel. This row proves routing only.
Preserves one source-stated signature-block signing act and signing capacity.
Records accountable omissions for expected state-AG settlement carrier families. It is not an answer substitute.
